acceptable.label: Acceptable to Use
acceptable.description: This AI service appears consistent with institutional policy. Follow standard data handling practices.

review.label: Review Required
review.description: This use case requires additional review before proceeding. Contact your department's IT liaison or the Office of Information Security.

notAcceptable.label: Not Acceptable
notAcceptable.description: This AI service or use case does not meet institutional policy requirements. Do not proceed. Contact the Office of Information Security for guidance.

questions.text: Will this AI service process, store, or transmit data classified as Restricted or Highly Sensitive (e.g., SSNs, medical records, financial account data, authentication credentials)?
questions.hint: Examples: HIPAA-covered health data, payment card data, Social Security numbers, passwords.

questions.text: Will this AI service process personally identifiable information (PII) such as student records, employee data, or other data covered by FERPA, GDPR, or similar regulations?
questions.hint: Examples: student grades, employee HR records, EU resident personal data.

questions.text: Is this AI service provided by a vendor that has NOT been reviewed and approved by IT Procurement or the Office of Information Security?
questions.hint: Check the approved vendor list if unsure. New or unknown tools typically require review.

questions.text: Will the output of this AI service be used to make or significantly influence high-stakes decisions about individuals (e.g., hiring, admissions, grading, medical treatment)?
questions.hint: AI-assisted decisions affecting people's opportunities, rights, or wellbeing require human oversight.

questions.text: Does this AI service require sharing institutional proprietary information, trade secrets, or unpublished research data with a third-party system?
questions.hint: Consider whether the data leaving institutional control could cause competitive or legal harm.

questions.text: Is this AI service being used in a context where inaccurate or hallucinated output could cause harm, legal liability, or significant institutional risk if not reviewed by a human?
questions.hint: Examples: legal documents, clinical guidance, financial reporting, public communications.

questions.text: Will this AI service be integrated into a production system or automated workflow without human review of its outputs?
questions.hint: Fully automated AI pipelines require additional security and accuracy review.
